title: Post Match Thread: Chelsea 5-1 Tottenham Hotspur [Premier League]
author: StamfordBridgeCat
body:
Chelsea 5-1 Tottenham

Goals: Palmer 12', 55' — Estêvão 34', 71' — João Pedro 63' — Kolo Muani 89' (TOT)

Red card: Romero 38' (TOT)

GET IN. Discuss the demolition below. Be nice to visiting Spurs fans (don't).

comments.author: CFCDanny
comments.text: I've been on this sub through Sarri-ball discourse, the Kepa penalty incident, and the 2023 season we do not mention. I have EARNED tonight. WE ARE SO BACK.

replies.author: BluesTilIDie_Sue
replies.text: Enjoy it tonight Danny but I'm legally obligated to remind you we said this before the Arsenal game too.

replies.author: CFCDanny
replies.text: Sue I love you but if you bring up the Emirates one more time tonight I'm calling the mods.

comments.author: xG_Marcus
comments.text: Quick numbers before the full breakdown tomorrow: 3.8 xG is our best single-match figure of the season, and it was already 2.1 BEFORE the red card. This wasn't just "they had 10 men". The press was suffocating from minute one.

replies.author: ZolaWasMyDad
replies.text: Marcus posting W stats for once instead of consoling us with 'the underlying numbers are good actually'. Character development.

comments.author: KTBFFH_1905
comments.text: Romero spent 38 minutes trying to kick Estêvão and then got sent off for it. There's a lesson in there about touching hot stoves.

comments.author: ShedEndTerry
comments.text: Brilliant, yes, well done everyone. But we're all just ignoring Delap's miss at 5-1? Standards win titles. Downvote me all you want.

replies.author: CarefreeChloe
replies.text: Terry it's a 5-1 derby win. Go and have a biscuit and be happy for one (1) evening.

comments.author: GoonerGaz_
comments.text: 10 men for an hour and you're throwing a parade lmao. Arsenal did you 3-0 with 11 last week. Anyway.

replies.author: KTBFFH_1905
replies.text: Sir this is the Chelsea sub. Also we knocked you out of the Carabao. Door's that way.

title: [Ornstein] Chelsea in advanced talks to extend Cole Palmer's contract to 2031 — club view him as 'cornerstone' of the Rea project
author: KTBFFH_1905
body:
Per Ornstein at The Athletic: significantly improved terms, announcement possible before the January window opens.

Cornerstone. Our cold little geezer, contracted until he's 29. Feels good man.

comments.author: CFCDanny
comments.text: Palmer to 2031, Maignan talks, Fofana out for— okay two out of three isn't bad.

comments.author: CarefreeChloe
comments.text: The fact that top clubs were sniffing around in the summer and he's signing UP instead of agitating out tells you everything about where this project is under Rea. This is the part of the rebuild nobody tweets about.

replies.author: BluesTilIDie_Sue
replies.text: I'll allow this optimism because it's contractually binding.

comments.author: ZolaWasMyDad
comments.text: 2031. My daughter will be in secondary school and Palmer will still be doing the celebration. Beautiful.

title: [Romano] Chelsea open talks with AC Milan for Mike Maignan — Milan want €35m, player keen
author: KTBFFH_1905
body:
Here we go soon? Romano says personal terms won't be an issue.

Genuine question for the sub: is this harsh on Sánchez? He's had a decent season by his standards. But Maignan is a different tier and everyone knows it.

comments.author: ShedEndTerry
comments.text: I have been posting about the goalkeeper situation on this forum since AUGUST. I have the receipts. I was called a boomer, a merchant, and (unrelated) banned for two days. Who's laughing now. MAIGNAN. IN.

replies.author: xG_Marcus
replies.text: Terry you were banned for calling a mod 'a Kepa apologist with a keyboard' but yes, on this specific issue, you were right.

comments.author: BluesTilIDie_Sue
comments.text: It's harsh on Sánchez and I don't care. World class keepers win you 8-10 points a season. Ask Arsenal about Raya. Announce him before I get emotionally invested, please, I know how this club operates.

comments.author: KepaApologist
comments.text: We really going to spend €35m on a 30-year-old keeper when we have perfectly good options in the building?

title: How Rea's 3-2-5 buildup dismantled the best press in the league — a breakdown (long)
author: xG_Marcus
body:
As promised, the full breakdown of Sunday.

1) Cucurella inverting next to Caicedo turned Spurs' man-to-man press into a 2v3 in our first phase, every single time. Their 10 didn't know whether to jump or hold.

2) Palmer's starting position was the smartest thing on the pitch: he kept dropping into the exact blind spot between their DM and RCB. Both his goals start from that pocket.

3) Estêvão stayed max-width the entire first half, which is WHY the Romero red happens: 40 metres of grass, one recovering defender, no cover. The red card wasn't luck, it was engineered.

4) PPDA 8.2 — we pressed them into oblivion even at 4-0.

This is the clearest tactical identity Chelsea have had in years. Rea deserves enormous credit for holding the plan after the Emirates.

comments.author: CarefreeChloe
comments.text: The point about the red being engineered rather than lucky is so real. We isolated their most card-prone defender against the fastest 18-year-old in Europe for 38 straight minutes. That's not gambling, that's setting a trap.

comments.author: ShedEndTerry
comments.text: Good post Marcus, I'll admit it. One question from a dinosaur: what happens when a team just goes long over the press and runs at Chalobah? Because that's exactly what Arsenal did.

replies.author: xG_Marcus
replies.text: Genuinely the right question, Terry. That's the Maignan link explained in one sentence — a keeper who dominates his box changes the maths on long balls. The rebuild is more joined-up than people think.

comments.author: ZolaWasMyDad
comments.text: I understood about 40% of this and upvoted anyway. #ReaBall

title: Fofana out for 'weeks' with a hamstring — official. Vent thread.
author: BluesTilIDie_Sue
body:
You know the drill by now. Scan done, "assessed over the coming weeks", which in Chelsea Medical Time means we'll see him at Easter.

He was genuinely playing the best football of his Chelsea career. I'm so tired. Vent below, keep it civil-ish.

comments.author: ZolaWasMyDad
comments.text: Fofana's hamstrings have their own entry in the injury table at this point. Genuinely heartbreaking though, he was immense in the derby before coming off.

comments.author: CFCDanny
comments.text: Silver lining dept: Acheampong gets minutes now and the kid has looked READY every time he's played. The rebuild feeds itself.

replies.author: BluesTilIDie_Sue
replies.text: I admire your ability to find the bright side within 45 minutes of official bad news. Teach me.

comments.author: KTBFFH_1905
comments.text: Romano already saying we're monitoring a loan CB with PL experience. For once the club is reacting BEFORE the crisis. Who are we and what have we done with Chelsea.

title: Unpopular opinion: Delap should start over João Pedro in both Newcastle legs
author: ShedEndTerry
body:
Hear me out before the downvotes. Cup semis in January are wars, not chess. St James' Park, freezing, long balls, set pieces. Delap bullies centre-halves in exactly those games and João Pedro can come off the bench when it opens up.

In my day you picked horses for courses. That's it. That's the post.

comments.author: CarefreeChloe
comments.text: Terry posting an actually reasonable take and framing it like he's about to be arrested for it is very funny to me. This is just squad rotation. Rea literally already does this.

replies.author: ShedEndTerry
replies.text: You lot have conditioned me to expect the worst, Chloe.

comments.author: xG_Marcus
comments.text: Data point in your favour: Delap's aerial win rate is 68% this season and João Pedro's pressing numbers dip in away games. This is a defensible take. I can't believe I'm agreeing with Terry twice in one week.

comments.author: CFCDanny
comments.text: Counterpoint: João Pedro has 3 in 4 and you don't bench form in a semi. NEXT.

title: Romero watching Estêvão receive the ball on the halfway line (colourised, 14 Dec 2025)
author: CarefreeChloe
body:
[Image description: the 'this is fine' dog, except the room isn't on fire yet — the match just kicked off]

Minute 12: this is fine
Minute 34: this is less fine
Minute 38: 🟥

comments.author: ZolaWasMyDad
comments.text: Estêvão has Romero's Chattr password at this point. Owns him digitally and physically.

comments.author: CFCDanny
comments.text: The scariest part is he's 18 and STILL on the 'don't overhype the kid' protocol. The protocol is failing. I am hyping.

replies.author: BluesTilIDie_Sue
replies.text: The protocol died at goal number two. Even I'm hyped and my heart has been medically classified as scar tissue since 2023.

comments.author: KepaApologist
comments.text: Petition to rename the halfway line 'Romero's Bermuda Triangle'.
